publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 165-169. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Thursday Night

Dearest William. I have had much satisfaction in my consultation with Dr L-- as to my case. Altho' he takes a somewhat different view from what he did; & I think anticipates that a period of some duration will be requisite. He is now trying an experiment upon me, which we hope will answer.-- Something or other
further is most clearly requisite; for I am far from improving, in several respects.-- Dr L-- says that I want watching for a time; & is very glad that I am to be now within reach.--
I am terribly tormented by smoke here;-- in fact it is quite filthy & odious. I have no peace with it; having only the choice of suffocation or starvation.
I have just returned from seeing the Merchant of Venice at Drury

Lane. And I cannot say how gratified I have been.-- Macready's Shylock is so excellent, that really you must see it. I am highly pleased with all I see & hear of his undertaking in that Theatre. He seems likely to raise the tone & style of our stage most laudably;-- & Shakespeare's plays have a chance of being worthily performed.--
Pray pity me, as I am at this moment; covered with showers of blacks, & unable to see distinctly even across my little sitting-room,
altho' I am shivering with the door open.--
Friday. 4 o'clock. My Dear! Do rejoice with the happy excited joyful little Avis.--

I am distracted with delight on 2 subjects. 1stly: the Artificial Ice, which I saw this morning. I am astonished and delighted beyond all measure. It seems decidedly preferable even to real Ice; & these are the testimonies of first-rate Skaters on the subject.--
It is proposed to lay down the floors of drawing-
-rooms, & other private dwelling apartments, in this material; a carpet being thrown over & taken up at pleasure. It want no repair at all scarcely.
It is expected to be a great resource in Country-Houses in the evenings .-- Mr Kirk is desirous to speak to you on business respecting their plans, with Lord Portman.
And I have ventured to promise for you that you will go to see the specimen on Thursday or Friday next.
But you must fix an hour for it, & let me know by return of post please.
Any hour either morning or night will suit them, that you choose to name.

It is in the New Road, [W? or nr? (abbreviation for near?)] Dorset Sqre, at Jenkins's Nursery Grounds. It is a subject of real importance; & when you see & hear all that I have, you will be as wild as I am about it;-- & as anxious, I am sure, to promote the

success of so delightful & beneficial a discovery.-- The 2 Mr Kirks & Mr Bradwell, all had much conversation with me; & are agreeable & highly gentlemanlike persons; seeming likewise to possess much taste & love of the Arts.
Then subject No 2, is my Singing. I have just had my first lesson; from a Signor Faia, a highly reputable & suitable person in all respects.-- He seems not a little pleased with me; & to anticipate
that I may even attain any degree of perfection; having a wonderful facility.
He is to come to Ockham twice, for 6 days each time.-- Mr Knoff & I could not hit it off together. But I think I have found exactly what I want.--

I am very glad that I have discovered an excellent restauranteur's; a very nice respectable Shop in Oxford St, where I can get either hot or cold breakfasts, dinners, or suppers, at a moment's notice.
It is the greatest possible convenience to me sometimes.
I am just starting for Esher. So Good Bye.
